service.description: Azure Virtual WAN appears across 9 normalized findings in 1 checklist families. 1 GA-ready families can anchor the baseline.
service.whatThisMeans: This service has a usable GA-ready baseline. Start there before branching into broader cross-service guidance.

quality.label: GA · High confidence
quality.summary: Strong review accelerator. Quality score 91 with 78% metadata completeness and 100% severity coverage.

items.text: Assign at least a /23 prefix to virtual hubs to ensure enough IP space is available.
items.graph: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.network/virtualhubs' | extend addressSpace = properties.addressPrefix | extend compliant= (toint(substring(addressSpace, indexof(addressSpace, '/') + 1)) < 23) | distinct name, id, compliant

items.text: For outbound Internet traffic protection and filtering, deploy Azure Firewall in secured hubs.
items.graph: resources | where type=='microsoft.network/virtualhubs' | extend compliant = isnotnull(properties.azureFirewall.id) | project id, compliant

items.text: Configure label-based propagation in Virtual WAN, otherwise connectivity between virtual hubs will be impaired.
Do not disable branch-to-branch traffic in Virtual WAN, unless these flows should be explicitly blocked.
items.graph: resources| where type =~ 'microsoft.network/virtualwans' | extend compliant= (properties.allowBranchToBranchTraffic == 'true') | distinct id,compliant

items.text: Ensure that your virtual WAN network architecture aligns to an identified architecture scenario.
Use a Virtual WAN hub per Azure region to connect multiple landing zones together across Azure regions via a common global Azure Virtual WAN.
Use AS-Path as hub routing preference, since it is more flexible than ExpressRoute or VPN.
items.graph: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.network/virtualhubs'| extend compliant= (properties.hubRoutingPreference =~ 'ASPath') | distinct id,compliant

items.text: Use Azure Monitor Insights for Virtual WAN to monitor the end-to-end topology of the Virtual WAN, status, and key metrics.
Use Virtual WAN if your scenario is explicitly described in the list of Virtual WAN routing designs.
